scene: Stan the round-headed dot-eyed stickman holding up a finger with a big empty thought bubble over his head, flat soft blue, big black caps GUESS YOUR NEXT THOUGHT.
Stan straining to guess, the thought bubble still empty with a question mark inside, flat soft blue, big black caps NOT THINK IT, GUESS IT.
Stan shrugging beside his still-empty thought bubble, flat soft blue, big black caps YOU CANT, CAN YOU.
A fully-formed thought popping into Stan's bubble out of nowhere with motion lines, flat soft blue, big black caps IT JUST SHOWS UP.
A thought drifting into Stan's head through a little open door like it wandered from another room, flat grey.
Stan with a sandwich suddenly appearing in his thought bubble, flat soft blue, big black caps A RANDOM SANDWICH.
Stan pointing at the sandwich thought with a big red question mark, flat soft blue, big black caps WHERE FROM.
Stan sitting at a writing desk labeled AUTHOR with a quill, looking unsure, flat grey, big black caps THE AUTHOR.
The voice in Stan's head drawn as a separate speech bubble while Stan listens warily, flat grey, big black caps NEVER THE SAME.
Stan in a thinker pose tapping his head, flat grey, big black caps I DO THE THINKING.
A tiny Stan drawn as a boss sitting at a desk inside his own big head, flat grey, big black caps THE BOSS UP HERE.
Stan placing thoughts one by one onto a little conveyor belt, flat grey, big black caps ONE AFTER ANOTHER.
An old song note, a worry cloud, and a question all drifting uninvited into Stan's head, flat soft blue, big black caps NOT INVITED.
A cringe memory labeled 2009 popping up uninvited while Stan winces, flat soft blue, big black caps IT ARRIVED.
Stan with a challenging grin and crossed arms, flat grey, big black caps PROVE IT.
A big white bear standing front and center while Stan is told not to think of it, flat soft blue, big black caps DON'T THINK OF A WHITE BEAR.
The white bear refusing to leave Stan's thought bubble, flat soft blue, big black caps AND THERE IT IS.
Stan unable to push the white bear out of his head, flat soft blue, big black caps YOU CANT NOT.
An old-timey Dostoevsky figure daring his brother, a small white bear between them, flat sepia, big black caps 200 YEARS AGO.
A lab-coat psychologist confirming it with a clipboard, a white bear on a chart, flat grey, big black caps CONFIRMED IN THE LAB.
A tug of war: the harder Stan pushes a thought away, the harder it shoves back, flat grey, big black caps IT SHOVES BACK.
Two boxes labeled CANT SUMMON and CANT BANISH over a stuck Stan, flat grey.
An empty throne labeled IN CHARGE with thoughts swirling out of Stan's control, flat grey, big black caps RUNNING THE SHOW.
A big red question mark over the whole setup, flat grey, big black caps NEITHER IS EASY.
Stan caught in a loop realizing you cannot think a thought before you think it, flat soft blue, big black caps BEFORE YOU THINK IT.
A chicken-and-egg loop of a thought trying to plan itself, flat grey.
A thought arriving from behind a curtain Stan cannot see past, flat grey, big black caps FROM SOMEWHERE UNSEEN.
Stan sitting as a reader watching thoughts scroll up a screen, not typing them, flat grey, big black caps YOU ARE THE READER.
Stan sitting doing nothing while his brain hums busily behind him, flat soft blue, big black caps EVEN DOING NOTHING.
A glowing brain network switching on, flat soft blue, big black caps THE DEFAULT MODE NETWORK.
The brain network blowing daydream bubbles the moment Stan stops concentrating, flat soft blue, big black caps IT JUST GENERATES.
Stan in a shower with a bright lightbulb idea ambushing him, flat blue, big black caps SHOWER THOUGHTS.
Stan on a walk being handed ideas he was not looking for, flat green, big black caps WHEN YOU STOP TRYING.
A pile of unsolicited thought-gifts handed to a surprised Stan, flat grey, big black caps THINGS YOU WEREN'T SEEKING.
Researchers pinging thousands of phones at random to ask where the mind is, flat blue, big black caps WHERE IS YOUR MIND.
A simple pie chart shaded almost half, a small Stan beside it, flat grey, big black caps 47 PERCENT.
Stan's body standing in one place while his see-through mind floats off elsewhere, flat soft blue, big black caps HALF YOUR LIFE.
Stan being carried off the ground by floating thought balloons he never chose, flat soft blue, big black caps THOUGHTS YOU NEVER CHOSE.
A red arrow pointing down from THOUGHTS to CHOICES, flat grey, big black caps DEEPER THAN THOUGHTS.
A 1980s scientist wiring Stan up to a brain scanner, flat grey, big black caps BENJAMIN LIBET, 1980s.
Stan wired up watching a fast clock on the wall, flat grey, big black caps WATCH THE CLOCK.
Stan flicking his wrist and noting the moment he decided, flat grey, big black caps FLICK YOUR WRIST.
A brain scanner lighting up before Stan even feels he decided, flat grey, big black caps BRAIN GEARS UP FIRST.
A simple timeline: brain activity, then a third of a second later the I-decided feeling, flat grey, big black caps A THIRD OF A SECOND.
The decision showing up in the brain first, the feeling arriving second, flat grey, big black caps THE FEELING CAME SECOND.
Stan being told about his own choice a beat late by a little messenger, flat grey, big black caps TOLD A BEAT LATE.
A big DEBATED rubber stamp pressed over the experiment, flat grey, big black caps FIERCELY DEBATED.
A tiny wrist-flick icon labeled SIMPLE MOVES ONLY beside Libet shaking his head, flat grey, big black caps SIMPLE MOVES ONLY.
Stan's conscious mind hitting a big VETO button on an impulse at the last instant, flat grey, big black caps THE VETO.
A hand-painted sign reading FREE WONT while Stan nods, flat soft blue, big black caps FREE WON'T.
An impulse arriving on its own from offstage into Stan's mind, flat grey, big black caps THE IMPULSE ARRIVES.
Stan at a fork in the road choosing what to do with the impulse, flat grey, big black caps YOUR PART.
Stan with a spinning head and a calming hand on his shoulder, flat soft blue, big black caps TAKE SOME COMFORT.
An old history scroll unrolling, flat sepia, big black caps WE DIDN'T EVEN KNOW.
An ancient Egyptian mummification scene, a hook up the nose pulling the brain out, flat sandy tan, big black caps THE BRAIN.
The brain being tossed into a trash basket, flat sandy tan, big black caps INTO THE TRASH.
A heart preserved carefully on a golden cloth, flat sandy tan, big black caps THE HEART, KEPT.
A heart on one side of a scale weighed against a single feather, flat sandy tan, big black caps WEIGHED VS A FEATHER.
An Egyptian figure pointing proudly at the heart as the seat of thought, flat sandy tan, big black caps THE SEAT OF THOUGHT.
An Aristotle figure calling the brain a wet sponge radiator that cools the blood, flat sepia, big black caps A RADIATOR.
Aristotle insisting real thought happens in the heart, flat sepia, big black caps THOUGHT IN THE HEART.
A chills beat: the ancient mistake still hiding in modern speech, flat grey, big black caps STILL HIDING.
BY HEART carved like a fossil into stone while Stan memorizes, flat brown, big black caps BY HEART.
HEARTFELT glowing warmly out of a heart, flat warm, big black caps HEARTFELT.
A row of old phrases trapped like little fossils in amber, flat brown, big black caps LITTLE FOSSILS.
Crowds across thousands of years all pointing at the heart for thinking, flat sepia, big black caps DEAD CERTAIN.
A brain generating the words while the heart gets all the credit in language, flat grey, big black caps NO CREDIT.
A quiet ME label glowing over Stan's calmly listening head, flat soft blue, big black caps THE QUIET TRUTH.
The narrating voice shown as separate from the source of thoughts, flat grey, big black caps NOT THE SOURCE.
Everything Stan has seen, heard, feared and loved churning in a dark cauldron beneath a surface line, flat dark navy, big black caps CHURNING BENEATH.
Thoughts recombining in the dark below the surface line, flat dark navy, big black caps IN THE DARK.
A finished thought being handed up to the surface while Stan takes the credit, flat soft blue, big black caps HANDED UP.
Stan as a reader turning pages he did not write, flat grey, big black caps THE READER, NOT THE WRITER.
The reader glowing as the most you there is, warm and gentle, flat warm, big black caps THE MOST YOU.
A heavy weight lifting off Stan's shoulders, a relieved smile, flat warm, big black caps ACTUALLY FREEING.
Stan lying awake in bed at 2:00 with a cruel intrusive thought bubble, flat dark navy, big black caps 2 AM.
An ugly random thought stamped NOT CHOSEN floating over Stan, flat dark navy, big black caps NOT CHOSEN.
The thought arriving on its own with the white bear callback beside it, flat soft blue, big black caps IT ARRIVED ON ITS OWN.
Stan calmly handing an ugly thought back, refusing shame, flat warm, big black caps DON'T HAVE TO BELIEVE IT.
A thought tag reading NEVER YOURS drifting away from Stan, flat soft blue, big black caps NEVER YOURS.
Stan calmly choosing what to do with a thought that came, flat warm, big black caps YOUR CHOICE.
A thought appearing out of nowhere while Stan finally sees it clearly for what it is, flat soft blue, big black caps SEE IT CLEARLY.
A message handed up from a deep unseen place inside Stan, flat grey, big black caps A MESSAGE FROM DEEP.
The message delivered to the one part of Stan that gets to read it and decide, flat soft blue, big black caps READ, AND DECIDE.
Stan who spent his whole life certain he did the thinking, flat grey, big black caps YOU THOUGHT YOU DID THE THINKING.
Final frame: Stan calmly and contentedly listening to the voice in his head, warm glow, flat warm, big black caps LUCKY ENOUGH TO LISTEN.
